figures.caption: Fig. 6. Success rate under different ρ values for Square and Mug tasks.

Fig. 1. GiB subtask evaluation pipeline. (A) A self-supervised model learns latent embeddings zi and assigns binary weights wi ∈ {0, 1} to trajectories, with wi = 1 indicating high-quality demonstrations. (B) Trajectories are segmented into k subtasks (e.g. k = 3), and for each subtask j, a Gaussian distribution of the latent trajectory N (µj ,Σj) is estimated using high-quality trajectories only. (C) Subtasks are evaluated by computing the Mahalanobis distance between each timestep’s latent embedding and the corresponding subtask distribution. The top ρ subtasks with the largest deviation scores are labeled as bad (shown in red), while the remaining ones are labeled as good (shown in green).
